documentPurpose: LLM-readable summary of the current ACS browser editor UI, edit functionality, screen structure, and major authoring workflows.

editorIdentity.navigationStyle: left-side workflow navigation with progressive disclosure by editor area

draftStorage.localDraftPersistence: browser persistence through draft key storage
draftStorage.projectPersistence: optional local API-backed project save/publish flow

coreRules: Most editing mutates one in-memory AdventurePackage draft, then rerenders the active workspace.
Validation is rerun locally after edits; server validation is optional through the local API.
Publishing/exporting works from immutable releases, not directly from the mutable draft.
Map Workspace tools are mode-sensitive and only show relevant controls for the selected layer mode.

screens.label: Adventure Setup
screens.purpose: Edit whole-adventure identity and draft-level metadata.
notes: This screen is currently metadata-focused rather than project-selection-focused.
The separate Validation panel is also visible in the adventure/test areas.

screens.label: World Atlas
screens.purpose: Edit high-level map structure, map selection, map hierarchy, and blank-map creation.
currentCapabilities: Select the current map from the atlas.
Change selected-map scale between World, Region, and Local Area.
Create a new blank map with dimensions and a fill tile.
knownCurrentQuirks: The screen still shows a relationship-stack diagram and both selected-map editing and create-map controls together.
Fill Tile is currently a free-text input, not a dropdown selector.
Parent Region is organizational metadata, not a travel restriction.

screens.label: Map Workspace
screens.purpose: Edit the currently selected map cell-by-cell.

layerModes.label: Terrain Tiles
currentCapabilities: Tile brush preview with swatch and label.

layerModes.label: Entity Instances
currentCapabilities: Place a new instance from a reusable entity definition.
Assign an optional per-instance display name.
Assign an optional per-instance behavior override.

layerModes.label: Trigger Markers
currentCapabilities: Attach the currently selected trigger to a map coordinate by clicking a cell.
Use the selected cell and trigger editor together as one workflow.

layerModes.label: Exits & Portals
currentCapabilities: Create or update a map exit by clicking a cell.
Inspect an existing exit by clicking its cell.
Set destination map and destination coordinates.

supportingBehavior: Selected Cell Inspector updates from the clicked coordinate.
Editor Hint text changes by mode.

screens.label: Libraries
screens.purpose: Manage reusable game-wide definitions, categories, and presentation assets.
screens.layoutModel: left-to-right drilldown with detail editor below/right

currentCapabilities: Edit entity definition name, kind, placement policy, asset id, faction, stats, skills, starting possessions, behavior mode, turn interval, detection, leash, and wander radius.
Create and edit quest objective objects.
Create and edit quest reward objects.
Set target map and target item on objectives.
Set reward kind and reward item.
Choose paint color from a palette selector with visible color preview.
Review in-game, magnified, and grouping sprite previews.

sharedCategoryCapabilities: Create category for current library focus.

screens.label: Logic & Quests
screens.purpose: Author structured triggers and their no-code rules.
currentCapabilities: Create, duplicate, and delete trigger records.
Set trigger type, map reference, coordinate, and run-once flag.
Build conditions without editing raw JSON.
Build actions without editing raw JSON.
Teleport, show dialogue, set flags, give items, play media cues, play sound cues, set quest stage, and change tiles from the no-code builder.
Inspect referenced maps, dialogue, items, quests, and tiles through the reference summary.

screens.label: Test & Publish
screens.purpose: Validate, projectize, publish, preview, export, diagnose, and reskin the edited adventure.
currentCapabilities: Run local validation and optional server validation.
Create a project from the draft through the local API.
Save the current draft into the project record.
Open the latest published release in play mode.
Compare editable versus play-only handoff artifacts.
Preview and apply player-facing rename/reskin changes.
Inspect authoring diagnostics and playtest scenarios.
artifactPreviewHighlights: Forkable preview shows release metadata, starter-pack preservation, custom library counts, recommended file name, and import guidance.

Standalone preview shows bundle entry file, download name, extracted folder name, runtime asset counts, launcher scripts, packaged readme files, release notes file, and the first shipped bundle files.
Release Readiness summarizes validation, release presence, preview state, handoff files, launcher readiness, and current known limitations.

screens.label: Validation
screens.purpose: Show draft health for the current draft in the adventure/test areas.
notes: This panel is shared across the adventure and test editor areas.

majorWorkflows.name: Edit adventure metadata
steps: Open Adventure Setup.
Edit Title and Description.
Draft mutates immediately and local validation is marked dirty.

majorWorkflows.name: Create and structure a map
steps: Open World Atlas.
Select an existing map or enter data in Create Map.
Set name, scale, parent region, width, height, and fill tile.
Create the blank map or update selected-map metadata.

majorWorkflows.name: Paint terrain
steps: Open Map Workspace.
Set Layer Mode to Terrain Tiles.
Choose a Tile Brush.
Click or drag across the grid to paint multiple cells.

majorWorkflows.name: Place or move an entity instance
steps: Open Map Workspace.
Set Layer Mode to Entity Instances.
Choose Place Definition to place a new instance or Move Instance to reposition an existing one.
Optionally assign an instance display name and behavior override.
Click a cell to place or move.

majorWorkflows.name: Attach trigger logic to a cell
steps: Open Logic and select or create a Trigger record.
Configure When / If / Then.
Switch to Map Workspace Trigger Markers mode.
Click a map cell to bind the selected trigger to that coordinate.

majorWorkflows.name: Author an exit or portal
steps: Open Map Workspace.
Set Layer Mode to Exits & Portals.
Choose target map and target coordinates.
Click a cell to create/update the exit or click an existing exit to inspect it.

majorWorkflows.name: Edit reusable library content
steps: Open Libraries.
Choose Library Type.
Review or create categories.
Review object summaries.
If the chosen type has a full editor today, edit details directly in the detail panel.

majorWorkflows.name: Publish and export
steps: Open Test & Publish.
Validate draft and create/save a project if needed.
Enter release label and release notes.
Publish a release.
Preview forkable or standalone artifact.
Export forkable JSON or standalone ZIP from the latest release.

progressiveDisclosureRules: Editor area navigation determines which large sections are active.
Map Workspace mode determines which tool controls are visible.
Library Type determines which category/object summaries and which detail editor are shown.
Artifact preview panels remain informative even before a release exists, but export buttons stay disabled until the release flow is valid.
currentStrengths: Mode-aware map editing with real progressive disclosure in the workspace toolbar.
Structured trigger authoring without requiring raw JSON by default.
Release-backed preview/export workflow for both editable and play-only distribution.
Shared validation, diagnostics, and playtest surfaces inside the editor.
currentLimitations: Editor still boots from a sample-first draft model rather than a true project/adventure hub.
World Atlas still mixes selected-map editing and create-map authoring in one screen and still uses a text input for Fill Tile.
Not every library type has a dedicated full detail editor yet; some are currently category and summary driven only.
Validation is still partly centralized in a shared Draft Health panel instead of being fully routed to the most relevant workspace.
User-facing terminology still includes Region-centric language even though future cleanup may shift the presentation toward World / Zone / Local Scene language.
relatedFutureWork: Milestone 36: editor completion, project-first Adventure Hub, WorldTree top-level information architecture, tooltip system, contextual validation, richer atlas cleanup, fuller CRUD coverage, and guided forkable import.
Milestone 37: richer player-facing front end and timed-action mode.
Milestone 38: renderer family choice at adventure creation time.
Milestone 39: broader starter-library and graphics completion.
